stemHtml: Over an aerodrome, there is frontal fog. What condition is likely to dissipate the fog?

options.a: After the front has passed there is no more cold air to cool the humid air close to the surface, the visibility should improve.
options.b: The descending air in the warm sector causes fog droplets to fall on the surface as dew, the visibility should improve.
options.c: The wind backs as the front passes, the air becomes drier and fog particles re-absorb into water vapour.
options.d: When the surface is heated in the cloudless sky immediately after the warm front has passed.
